mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of declining rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that immediately suppress consumer spending expectations and trigger negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >60% of instances where UNRATE rises above the 0.2pp threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real personal income combined with stable consumer prices indicates robust household purchasing power without inflationary pressure, driving positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative 20-day returns in >50% of occurrences, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth exceeding 0.5% monthly during late-cycle expansions confirms sustained manufacturing demand that supports corporate earnings and generates positive quarterly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Quarterly forward returns are negative in >55% of late-cycle periods where INDPRO growth exceeds 0.5%, or the signal predictive power vanishes outside late-cycle regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Increasing housing starts reflect expanding construction activity and rising consumer confidence, which directly boosts related sector revenues and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >55% of periods where HOUST increases month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: A consumer price index level exceeding 325 indicates accelerating inflation that forces central banks to tighten policy, compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in >60% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds the 325 threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in total nonfarm payrolls and declining unemployment rates confirms a strengthening labor market that increases wage growth expectations and drives positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields negative 60-day returns in >50% of occurrences, or the interaction effect is statistically insignificant in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Declining real personal income during a monetary tightening cycle signals eroding household balance sheets that reduce consumer discretionary spending and trigger negative monthly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in >55% of tightening periods where RSAFS declines, or the signal fails to predict returns outside tightening regimes.
